title: Improve podman docker image
body:
This PR will address #15451

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
No
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *StarpTech* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign edsantiago for approval by writing `/assign @edsantiago` in a comment. For more information see:[The Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Based on the changes, I opened a discussion on how to benefit from all storage features.

https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/15515
you need to sign your commit(s).  Please do a `git commit --amend -s`
@StarpTech unfortunately not enough,  You need to do the commit, fetch, rebase and push.  https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15514/checks?check_run_id=8052263971 for details.
@StarpTech Why not just update the existing Containerfiles to use fedora-minimal

> @StarpTech Why not just update the existing Containerfiles to use fedora-minimal

This would be a breaking change since many important binaries would be missing from the image, i.e. dnf.

> > @StarpTech Why not just update the existing Containerfiles to use fedora-minimal
> 
> This would be a breaking change since many important binaries would be missing from the image, i.e. dnf.

I don't consider it a breaking change as we do not give any promises on any packages being installed other than podman.  We should just not backport and override existing images.

I am also in favor of simply updating the existing Containerfiles rather than adding a new image.
@rhatdan @Luap99, can we settle on a way forward?

Consider this:
```
FROM quay.io/containers/podman
RUN dnf install <extra packages>
```
If somebody is using podman as base it will likely break them. I understand that we do not give any promises. If you think this is fine then do it.

@Luap99 Makes a good point.  How much size savings do we get and how can we fix the issue that dnf is not installed?

Isn't there an effort to make dnf not require python?, which would make microdnf and dnf the same?

> Isn't there an effort to make dnf not require python?

@fatherlinux may know

I begin to think that it's probably not worth risking a breaking change.  The size difference is smaller when downloading the images since the layers are compressed.  We have 38MiB for fedora-minimal and 68MiB for Fedora.

@Luap99 @rhatdan @StarpTech what do you think?
I agree this is not worth the risk.  We can look at this when/if dnf support gets added to fedora-minimal.
We've been reaching out to the Fedora folks who shared to plan dropping python dependencies of dnf with Fedora 38.
